tasks.direction_relation_note: Useful lifecycle follow-up once Phase 1 language and queue trust surfaces stop drifting.
tasks.title: Add external `akra doctor` after Phase 1 state and queue vocabulary stabilizes
tasks.description: Expose a non-interactive workspace-root command that reports planning state, queue summary, proposal summary, and the first blocking validation issue, but only after the operator-facing state and queue vocabulary is stable enough to avoid baking current wording churn into a public lifecycle entrypoint.
tasks.priority_reason: Still important for workspace lifecycle trust, but the latest direction read puts operator language and queue clarity ahead of lifecycle command expansion.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed initial canonical-language rollout for queue, automation overlay, and pause or recovery phrasing, establishing the shared operator projection path.
tasks.title: Make current state, cause, and next action the canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the first operator-language slice by adding a shared current-state/cause/next-action projection, switching queue and automation overlays to it, and rewriting key planning preview, status, and pause copy to use operator-facing terms without changing automation policy semantics.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit e20c70a and PR #238, so later work should build on this projection instead of reopening the same slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the shell-focused follow-up by landing the canonical one-line summary and aligning planning-control inspection labels with the snapshot preview taxonomy.
tasks.title: Extend canonical state language to the compact shell summary and remaining inspection surfaces
tasks.description: Completed the shell-focused rollout by switching the compact shell summary to the canonical current-state/cause/next-action line, aligning inline tail and planning-control labels with snapshot preview states, and updating the related shell/service tests in commit ab90d1c on PR #238.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit ab90d1c and pushed to PR #238, so follow-up work should move to the remaining automation preview surfaces instead of repeating the prior queue head.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Bootstraps the same default planning scaffold from the normal shell prompt.
tasks.title: Add external `akra init` for default planning scaffold creation
tasks.description: Expose a workspace-root bootstrap command that creates the default planning scaffold and refuses to overwrite existing active planning files, while reusing the shared planning bootstrap service instead of maintaining a second scaffold path.
tasks.priority_reason: The product should let operators bootstrap planning before opening the TUI, but this follows shared doctor visibility.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Defines safe reset targets and destructive-scope rules for long-lived planning workspaces.
tasks.title: Add shared reset semantics for `akra reset` and `:reset`
tasks.description: Define queue, directions, and full-workspace reset behavior behind shared planning services so reset paths clearly state rewritten artifacts, refuse unsafe directions-only resets with live tasks, and support both external and in-shell entrypoints without diverging rules.
tasks.priority_reason: Reset becomes important once doctor and init exist, but it must land with explicit safety semantics.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps `:doctor`, `:init`, and `:reset` aligned with the same external lifecycle model.
tasks.title: Wire `:doctor`, `:init`, and `:reset` to the shared planning lifecycle services
tasks.description: Add in-shell entrypoints that expose the same planning doctor, init, and reset semantics with richer overlays, staged review, or confirmation where needed, without creating a second planning lifecycle model inside the TUI.
tasks.priority_reason: Internal command parity matters, but shared services and external operators come first.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the queue workboard slice by shipping the new Now / Next / Proposed / Blocked inspection structure and validating proposal-only plus blocked or skipped states.
tasks.title: Reframe queue inspection as now, next, proposed, and blocked work
tasks.description: Completed the queue-facing projection rewrite by separating actionable queue head work into `Now`, follow-on executable items into `Next`, promotable candidates into `Proposed`, and skipped or blocked entries into `Blocked`, while keeping planner notices separate and adding regression coverage for proposal-only and blocked or skipped rendering in commit `6d341f8` after `cargo test` passed with 436 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit `6d341f8` after `cargo test` passed with 436 tests, so builtin next-task should advance to the next trust-surface expansion instead of repeating the same queue-head slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the inline-status follow-up by applying the queue workboard framing to automation preview or status, shell footer, and inline tail surfaces through a shared queue framing helper.
tasks.title: Extend now, next, proposed, and blocked framing to inline status surfaces
tasks.description: Completed the inline-status framing rollout by showing `Now / Next / Proposed / Blocked` directly in automation preview or status, reusing the same queue framing helper for shell footer and inline tail rendering, compacting proposal-only states down to proposal titles, and updating regression coverage in commit `45845af` after `cargo test` passed with 437 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit `45845af` after `cargo test` passed with 437 tests, so this queue head should close and the next compact status surfaces that still lack the queue framing should become the follow-up.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Direct continuation from the completed inline-status rollout so any remaining compact operator-visible status surfaces pick up the same executable queue framing instead of falling back to older summaries.
tasks.title: Extend now, next, proposed, and blocked framing to remaining compact status surfaces
tasks.description: Audit the compact status surfaces that still do not reuse the shared queue framing helper and extend `Now / Next / Proposed / Blocked` rendering to them, keeping proposal-only states compact and preserving one clear executable head outside the full queue inspector.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply says there is no remaining code in the shipped inline-status slice and identifies widening the same framing into other compact status surfaces as the next likely extension, making this the clearest executable follow-up to keep builtin next-task moving.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the automation-preview follow-up by moving preview rendering onto the shared canonical projection and removing the remaining legacy preview labels.
tasks.title: Align remaining automation preview labels with canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the automation preview rollout by replacing legacy `planning: ...` and `planning detail: ...` labels with direct `current state / cause / next action` rendering, wiring the preview service to reuse the runtime canonical projection, and updating overlay plus service tests in commit d412410 on PR #238.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit d412410 and pushed to PR #238, so the next queue head should move to the remaining legacy trust-surface wording instead of repeating this completed slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the remaining automation trust-surface cleanup by aligning automation metadata, queue notice, inline help, and planning simple review wording to the canonical operator language.
tasks.title: Audit and align remaining automation metadata and trust-surface legacy wording
tasks.description: Completed the final automation-focused wording audit by switching automation overlay and status metadata, inline command help, planning simple review, and queue notice copy to the canonical automation state / turn budget / stop keyword rule / thread context / last agent reply / Rendered Next-Turn Prompt language, removing the duplicated `planning notice: planning:` prefix, and updating the affected regression coverage in commit aef13d6 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance`.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit aef13d6 after `cargo test` passed with 417 tests, so the queue should move to the next explicitly suggested wording expansion instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the planner and startup-check slice by normalizing planner debug preview or panel labels, startup overlays, inline inspection, blocked prompts, and command-palette wording onto the canonical operator-facing language.
tasks.title: Align planner debug and diagnostic wording with the canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the planner debug and startup-check wording rollout by switching planner debug preview or panel labels to operator-facing terms such as planner state, queued work, planner update, and operator action, unifying startup overlays and inspection prompts around startup checks, and rewriting startup summaries to show current state / cause / next action directly with regression coverage in commit 4f845fa on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 419 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 4f845fa after `cargo test` passed with 419 tests, so the queue should move to the next explicitly suggested wording expansion instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the auxiliary debug-surface follow-up by extending the canonical operator wording into auto-follow transcript debug detail, queue overlay failure or loading summaries, and inline operator notices outside the planner and startup slices.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording to remaining auxiliary debug surfaces
tasks.description: Completed the auxiliary debug-surface wording rollout by switching auto-follow transcript debug detail to `planner session`, `planner update`, `Submitted Prompt`, and `Planner Reply`, rewriting queue overlay loading or failed states into direct current state / cause / next action summaries, renaming inline `detail:` notices to `operator action:`, and updating the related regression coverage in commit c6730ec on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 422 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit c6730ec after `cargo test` passed with 422 tests, so the queue should advance to the next explicitly suggested wording expansion instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the general-copy follow-up by extending the canonical wording into shell non-debug state copy and recent session state summaries, leaving only narrower residual copy candidates for selected-session metadata or other shell surfaces.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording beyond debug surfaces
tasks.description: Completed the general-copy rollout by updating shell and recent-session non-debug state summaries to use the canonical current state / cause / next action wording, rewriting prompt-line waiting or blocked language, aligning failure and retry or draft actions, and adding regression coverage in commit 6171625 on the current branch after `cargo test` passed with 425 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 6171625 after `cargo test` passed with 425 tests, so the next queue head should move to the narrower residual copy candidates instead of repeating this completed wording slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the selected-session metadata and residual shell-copy follow-up by extending canonical operator wording into recent-session selected detail labels and test-shell loading or failure summaries.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording to selected-session metadata and residual shell copy
tasks.description: Completed the selected-session metadata wording rollout by renaming recent-session selected detail labels to `thread id`, `last updated`, `thread source`, `current state`, `latest preview`, and `session file`, aligning test shell loading or failed views around `waiting` or `blocked` plus `current state / cause / next action`, and adding regression coverage in commit 86f64f9 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 427 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 86f64f9 after `cargo test` passed with 427 tests, so the queue should move to the narrower residual wording candidates named in the same reply instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the selected-session row and framed-shell prompt/help follow-up by extending canonical operator wording into recent-session row fields, framed shell headers, and prompt/help state summaries.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording to selected-session list rows and leftover shell copy
tasks.description: Completed the selected-session row wording rollout by switching recent-session rows to `thread`, `current state`, `last updated`, and `workspace`, using the thread title in active framed shell headers, normalizing framed shell prompt/help copy around `current state / cause / next action`, and updating regression coverage in commit 95688a7 on the current branch after `cargo test` passed with 429 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 95688a7 after `cargo test` passed with 429 tests, so the queue should move to the narrower residual non-inline shell copy candidates instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the framed-shell transcript follow-up by extending the canonical operator wording into transcript titles, empty placeholder state lines, shell or status titles, and frontend summary copy, leaving only optional residual cleanup outside this framed-shell slice.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording to framed shell transcript and residual non-inline copy
tasks.description: Completed the framed-shell transcript wording rollout by rewriting empty transcript placeholders to show `current state / cause / next action`, aligning framed transcript titles around `Conversation / ready|waiting|blocked`, normalizing shell or status titles plus frontend summary lines to operator-facing wording, and updating regression coverage in commit c881256 on the current branch after `cargo test` passed with 431 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit c881256 after `cargo test` passed with 431 tests, so the queue should advance to the last explicitly suggested residual cleanup slice instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the final shell-rendering residual slice by aligning the remaining inspection titles, section labels, session list or detail headings, and popup helper copy to the canonical operator wording.
tasks.title: Extend canonical operator wording to residual inspection titles and test-only helper copy
tasks.description: Completed the residual shell-rendering wording cleanup by changing inline or popup inspection titles to `operator inspection`, section framing to `Current State, Cause, and Next Action`, session list or detail headings to `Operator Status` and `Operator Actions`, and adding regression coverage for popup helper copy in commit 6b9a359 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 432 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 6b9a359 after `cargo test` passed with 432 tests, so the queue should advance to the broader optional inspection or header copy expansion named in the same reply instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the non-shell-rendering inspection and header-helper follow-up by aligning overlay-builder inspection titles, planning-direction headers, and test-only shell helper copy with the canonical operator wording, leaving only optional broader help or footer wording expansion.
tasks.title: Audit non-shell-rendering inspection and header helper copy for canonical operator wording
tasks.description: Completed the non-shell-rendering audit by renaming overlay-builder `shell inspection` or `shell guidance` titles to `operator inspection`, aligning planning and directions headers to `Planning Setup`, `Planning Draft`, `Direction Maintenance`, and `Direction Draft`, normalizing test-only shell header helper wording around `operator surface` and `transcript source`, and refreshing regression coverage in commit 8806067 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 8806067 after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests, so the queue should advance to the only remaining optional wording extension named in the same reply instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the general help and footer-helper follow-up by extending canonical operator wording into shared command hints, footer shorthand, inline tails, and planning automation helper copy, leaving only optional broader command-status or short-status wording outside the footer or helper slice.
tasks.title: Audit general help copy and footer helper wording for canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the general help and footer-helper audit by renaming `Shell commands` to `Operator commands`, aligning command hints and footer shorthand around `operator status`, `operator notice`, and `operator prompt`, expanding abbreviated help copy such as `Enter sends` and `Ctrl+j newline`, updating the planning automation status helper, and refreshing the affected regression coverage in commit ac5a367 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit ac5a367 after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests, so the queue should advance to the next explicitly suggested residual copy audit instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the command-status follow-up by normalizing operator command execution state labels and residual short status copy, leaving only an optional wider pass over non-status instructional hints.
tasks.title: Audit command execution status and residual short status copy for canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the short-status audit by aligning `:diag`, `:sessions`, `:queue`, and `:auto` execution state lines around `operator surface: ...`, rewriting planning setup or directions or editor entry statuses plus automation, thread, and startup shorthand to the canonical label-first language such as `automation state`, `turn budget`, `draft workspace`, and `startup checks ready`, and confirming the rollout with `cargo test` in commit f0a83c3 on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after 433 tests passed.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit f0a83c3 after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests, so the queue should advance to the only optional broader copy pass named in the same reply instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the short-instructional-hint follow-up by replacing shorthand buffered-hint, overlay legend, and planning setup or review or editor helper text with canonical next-action or controls wording, leaving only optional non-hint compact status phrasing outside the help surfaces.
tasks.title: Audit residual short instructional hints for canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the short instructional-hint audit by rewriting inline command buffered hints plus overlay or help legends from `Press Enter ...` or `Enter: ...` shorthand into canonical `next action:` and `controls:` sentences, extending the same pattern across automation, recent sessions, startup checks, queue, planning setup or review or editor, and directions maintenance surfaces, and updating the related render or surface expectations in commit c6528f1 on the current branch after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit c6528f1 after `cargo test` passed with 433 tests, so the queue should move to the only narrower residual copy pass named in the same reply instead of repeating this completed head task.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the last residual operator-language slice by aligning planning draft save or open or promote or bootstrap states plus directions maintenance status labels and draft-editor overlay fields with the canonical operator wording.
tasks.title: Audit residual compact non-hint status copy for canonical operator language
tasks.description: Completed the residual compact non-hint audit by rewriting planning draft save or open or promote or bootstrap statuses around `staged draft`, `validation state`, `promoted files`, `cause`, and `next action`, aligning planning draft editor overlay labels to `staged draft`, `current file`, and `validation state`, normalizing directions maintenance overview or selection or confirm labels to `direction coverage`, `queue idle rule`, `direction parsing`, `current selection`, `current direction`, and `current state`, and updating the related shell rendering, surface, and planning runtime tests in commit 5ffaa2d on `feature/native-operator-state-language-canonical-guidance` after `cargo test` passed with 434 tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Finished in main session commit 5ffaa2d after `cargo test` passed with 434 tests, so the queue should move out of the operator-language cleanup stream and onto the next explicitly prioritized queue-trust slice.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Third active stream after state language and queue trust, consistent with the latest operator-facing priority summary.
tasks.title: Simplify first-run planning review around the simple-mode happy path
tasks.description: Refocus planning onboarding, staged review copy, and queue-idle explanation so a new workspace reaches its first successful queue-driven continuation with minimal setup friction, after the shell and queue already use stable operator-facing language.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest directions summary names planning authoring ergonomics as the third immediate workstream once state and queue clarity are in place.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Connects resumed conversations to the current work-management state.
tasks.title: Surface planning and queue context immediately after session resume
tasks.description: Ensure resumed sessions show whether planning is active, what the current queue summary is, and whether automation can continue without forcing the operator to open multiple overlays.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Architecture follow-up that unlocks safer UX iteration for Phase 1 and Phase 2.
tasks.title: Extract a status and projection layer from shell presentation hotspots
tasks.description: Separate operator wording and status projection from layout and runtime plumbing so future UX changes can land in smaller, safer slices.
tasks.priority_reason: Needed to keep follow-up UX changes reviewable and decoupled from layout churn.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Preserves release confidence while operator surfaces evolve.
tasks.title: Codify terminal validation expectations for Phase 1 operator-surface work
tasks.description: Update validation and operator runbook flows so shell, queue, and planning UX changes always record the real-terminal checks that were actually run.
